hierarchy.label: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd.

extraction_hints.snippet: **Date of Judgment:** November 14, 2025
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd.

extraction_hints.snippet: This is an action by Hargrove Consulting Inc. ("Plaintiff") against Thornfield Logistics Ltd. ("Defendant") for breach of a consulting services agreement dated March 1, 2024, and for damages arising from the Defendant's wrongful termination of that agreement
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Introduction

extraction_hints.snippet: The Plaintiff claims damages of $485,000 in unpaid fees and $120,000 for lost profits following wrongful termination

extraction_hints.snippet: I find that the Defendant's termination on September 15, 2024, was wrongful

extraction_hints.snippet: The agreement required 30 days prior written notice of any performance concern before a notice of termination could issue
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Liability

extraction_hints.snippet: Applying Renwick Building Products Ltd. v. Carleton Designs Corp., 2018 ONCA 227, the Defendant's termination was in breach of the express terms of the agreement

extraction_hints.snippet: Applying Renwick Building Products Ltd. v. Carleton Designs Corp., 2018 ONCA 227, the Defendant's termination was in breach of the express terms of the agreement
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Liability

extraction_hints.snippet: Carleton Designs Corp., 2018 ONCA 227, the Defendant's termination was in breach of the express terms of the agreement
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Liability

extraction_hints.snippet: The Plaintiff is also entitled to damages for lost profits for the unexpired portion of the 24-month term
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Damages

extraction_hints.snippet: Applying the principles in Fidler v. Sun Life Assurance Co. of Canada, 2006 SCC 30, lost profits must be calculated on the balance of the contract term, less amounts the Plaintiff reasonably mitigated or could reasonably have mitigated
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Damages

extraction_hints.snippet: The Plaintiff obtained a replacement contract worth $6,000 per month commencing December 1, 2024
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Damages

extraction_hints.snippet: The unexpired portion of the agreement ran from September 15, 2024 to February 28, 2026, a period of 17.5 months
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Analysis > Damages

extraction_hints.snippet: General damages of $485,000 for unpaid fees
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Order

extraction_hints.snippet: Damages for wrongful termination of $190,000
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Order

extraction_hints.snippet: Pre-judgment interest from September 15, 2024, at the rate prescribed under the Courts of Justice Act, R.S.O
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Order

extraction_hints.snippet: Costs of the action on a partial indemnity basis, to be assessed if not agreed
extraction_hints.context_heading: Hargrove Consulting Inc. v. Thornfield Logistics Ltd. > Order

events.description: This is an action by Hargrove Consulting Inc. ("Plaintiff") against Thornfield Logistics Ltd. ("Defendant") for breach of a consulting services agreement dated March 1, 2024, and for damages arising from the Defendant's wrongful termination of that agreement
The Plaintiff commenced this action on June 12, 2024
The Defendant filed its Statement of Defence on August 5, 2024
A motion for summary judgment was heard on January 20, 2025
The motion was dismissed on March 3, 2025, the court finding that genuine issues of material fact remained to be tried
Pre-trial conference was held on May 8, 2025
The trial was heard over four days from October 6 to October 9, 2025
The Plaintiff entered into a consulting services agreement with the Defendant on March 1, 2024, for a term of 24 months
On September 15, 2024, the Defendant sent a letter purporting to terminate the agreement with immediate effect, citing alleged unsatisfactory performance
On September 18, 2024, the Plaintiff wrote to the Defendant disputing the termination and demanding reinstatement or payment of the outstanding amounts
On October 1, 2024, the Defendant engaged a replacement contractor without notifying the Plaintiff
Invoices submitted by the Plaintiff for services rendered between March 1 and September 14, 2024, totalling $485,000, were never paid

deadlines.description: Written submissions were received by October 24, 2025
The agreement required 30 days prior written notice of any performance concern before a notice of termination could issue

obligations.description: The Defendant shall pay General damages of $485,000 for unpaid fees
The Defendant shall pay Damages for wrongful termination of $190,000
The Defendant shall pay Pre-judgment interest from September 15, 2024
The Defendant shall pay Costs of the action on a partial indemnity basis, to be assessed if not agreed

conditions.description: Carleton Designs Corp., 2018 ONCA 227, which confirms that a party may terminate only in accordance with the express terms of the agreement or upon a repudiatory breach by the other party

legal_authorities.citation: Victoria Laundry (Windsor) Ltd. v. Newman Industries Ltd
Transamerica Life Insurance Co. of Canada v. Canada Life Assurance Co, 1996 CanLII 7979 (ON SC)
